generated_oml:
// C3: Physical sensing components
instance TemperatureSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Temperature sensor modelled as a device object in the BIM; publishes temperature measurements to Eclipse Hono"
    DTDFVocab:producedData TemperatureDataTransmitted
]

instance HumiditySensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Humidity sensor modelled as a device object in the BIM; publishes humidity measurements to Eclipse Hono"
    DTDFVocab:producedData HumidityDataTransmitted
]

instance OccupancySensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Occupancy (post‑occupancy) sensor modelled as a device object in the BIM; publishes occupancy counts to Eclipse Hono"
    DTDFVocab:producedData OccupancyDataTransmitted
]

// C4: Physical-to-virtual interaction – Data transmitted
instance TemperatureDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom TemperatureSensor
]

instance HumidityDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom HumiditySensor
]

instance OccupancyDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom OccupancySensor
]

// C10: Data instances
instance TemperatureData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Near‑real‑time temperature measurements streamed from the physical sensor"
    DTDFVocab:fromData TemperatureDataTransmitted
    DTDFVocab:inputTo EclipseDitto
]

instance HumidityData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Near‑real‑time humidity measurements streamed from the physical sensor"
    DTDFVocab:fromData HumidityDataTransmitted
    DTDFVocab:inputTo EclipseDitto
]

instance OccupancyData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Near‑real‑time occupancy counts streamed from the physical sensor"
    DTDFVocab:fromData OccupancyDataTransmitted
    DTDFVocab:inputTo EclipseDitto
]

// C2: Acting components
instance HVACActuator : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Physical HVAC actuator defined in the BIM and exposed through Eclipse Ditto for remote control"
]

instance LightingActuator : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Physical lighting actuator defined in the BIM and exposed through Eclipse Ditto for remote control"
]

// C11: Enablers (middleware)
instance EclipseHono : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Eclipse Hono middleware that receives sensor messages and forwards them to Eclipse Ditto"
    DTDFVocab:enables RealTimeInsightGenerationService,
                 SimulationAnalysisService,
                 PredictiveAnalyticsService,
                 OptimisationSupportService
]

instance EclipseDitto : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Eclipse Ditto twin engine that stores the digital twin state and executes control‑logic"
    DTDFVocab:enables RealTimeInsightGenerationService,
                 SimulationAnalysisService,
                 PredictiveAnalyticsService,
                 OptimisationSupportService
]

// C6: Services
instance RealTimeInsightGenerationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Generates real‑time operational insights from incoming sensor data"
    DTDFVocab:provides RealTimeMonitoringInsight
]

instance SimulationAnalysisService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Runs simulation‑based analysis using the semantic and geometric models"
    DTDFVocab:provides SimulationOutcomeInsight
]

instance PredictiveAnalyticsService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Performs predictive analytics to forecast building performance and failures"
    DTDFVocab:provides PredictiveFailureInsight
]

instance OptimisationSupportService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Provides optimisation recommendations to reduce operational costs"
    DTDFVocab:provides OptimisationRecommendationInsight
]

// C17: Insights
instance RealTimeMonitoringInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Continuous monitoring insight showing current temperature, humidity and occupancy status"
]

instance SimulationOutcomeInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Insight derived from simulated scenarios (e.g., energy consumption under different setpoints)"
]

instance PredictiveFailureInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Forecast of potential equipment failures based on historical trends and current sensor data"
]

instance OptimisationRecommendationInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Actionable recommendation to adjust HVAC or lighting settings for energy optimisation"
]

// C15: Actions (derived from insights)
instance AdjustHVACAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Facility manager can issue a command to the HVAC actuator to change temperature setpoint"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic false
]

instance AdjustLightingAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Facility manager can issue a command to the lighting actuator to dim or switch off lights"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic false
]

// C10: Models / Data
instance BIMGeometricModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "BIM geometric model providing the 3‑D building representation"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo EclipseDitto
]

instance EnrichedSemanticModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Semantic model enriched with sensor, actuator and controller definitions extracted from BIM via MDE"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo EclipseDitto
]

instance ControlLogicModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Executable control‑logic model generated from BIM annotations for runtime execution"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo EclipseDitto
]

// C9: Environment (centralised DT hub per building)
instance BuildingTwinEnvironment : DTDFVocab:Environment [
    base:contains TemperatureSensor,
                HumiditySensor,
                OccupancySensor,
                HVACActuator,
                LightingActuator,
                BIMGeometricModel,
                EnrichedSemanticModel,
                ControlLogicModel,
                EclipseHono,
                EclipseDitto
]

instance virtual_to_physical_interaction : DTDFVocab:VirtualToPhysical [
    base:desc "Bidirectional control is realized by the Digital Twin issuing actuation commands back to the building control systems through the Ditto platform, which routes the commands via Eclipse Hono to the physical actuators. Command types include set‑point adjustments, on/off switches, and schedule updates. Messages follow the Ditto command format (JSON‑based) and are validated by the runtime configuration management layer before transmission. Authentication and integrity checks are enforced by the middleware’s built‑in security mechanisms. Feedback from the physical layer (acknowledgments and status reports) is sent back to the twin, providing execution confirmation and enabling conflict resolution when multiple control requests occur."
]

instance twinning_time_scale : DTDFVocab:TimeScale [
    base:desc "The framework provides real‑time data exchange, meaning sensor and actuator information from the physical building is streamed continuously to the digital twin and vice‑versa. Synchronization is described as continuous with near‑instantaneous latency, supporting real‑time monitoring and control; no explicit numerical frequencies or granularity are specified in the document."
]

instance dt_constellation : DTDFVocab:Constellation [
    base:desc "The system follows a modular, service‑oriented constellation: a central DT Framework provides standardized extension points, runtime configuration management, and integrates custom services and analytics. Roles such as Building Control Engineers, Facility Managers, and DT Engineers interact with the framework, which orchestrates data flow between enriched BIM models and operational twins across the building life‑cycle."
]

instance fidelity_and_validity_considerations : DTDFVocab:FidelityConsideration [
    base:desc "The workflow includes a validation step after the MDE transformation, where the generated twin is checked for semantic correctness. Quality assurance is addressed through the Overall Digital Twin Entanglement (ODTE) metric, which quantifies the alignment between the physical building and its digital counterpart. The model‑driven approach promotes compliance, reuse, and systematic verification, but the document does not describe specific uncertainty quantification, error‑detection, or correction mechanisms beyond the ODTE evaluation."
]

instance dt_technical_connection : DTDFVocab:TechnicalConnection [
    base:desc "The technical backbone consists of a central DT Framework that connects physical devices and the digital twin via Eclipse Hono and Eclipse Ditto. Hono provides protocol support (e.g., MQTT/HTTP) for IoT device connectivity, while Ditto offers REST/WebSocket interfaces for twin interaction. The network topology follows a hub‑spoke model with the DT Framework as the hub, ensuring low‑latency, high‑throughput data exchange suitable for real‑time synchronization. Redundancy is achieved through the modular architecture’s standardized extension points, allowing fail‑over components. Security protocols are applied at the middleware layer to protect data in transit."
]

instance dt_hosting_deployment : DTDFVocab:Deployment [
    base:desc "Deployment follows a model‑driven approach where enriched BIM models are used to instantiate operational digital twins. The framework’s modular design and runtime configuration management allow components to be deployed and adjusted without full redeployment, supporting scalability and maintainability. However, the document does not provide details on hosting platforms (cloud, edge, on‑premise), computational or storage specifications, explicit scalability mechanisms, supported deployment models, or availability/reliability guarantees."
]

instance horizontal_integration : DTDFVocab:HoriIntegration [
    base:desc "Horizontal integration is addressed through generic interoperability requirements, standardized extension points, and the use of BIM tools as data sources. While the document emphasizes interoperability across heterogeneous systems and integration of custom services, it does not specify concrete protocols or standards (e.g., MQTT, OPC-UA). Integration relies on the framework’s modular APIs and runtime configuration to interoperate with external systems and enterprise services."
]
